1â†’# CLI Subcommand Review: Findings & Cleanup Plan
     2â†’
     3â†’## Context
     4â†’
     5â†’Reviewed all 5 subcommands (`collect`, `augment`, `search`, `compile`, `all`) plus 2 legacy workflows (`--dataset-dir` DatasetSplitsWorkflow, AnalyzerWorkflow). Found significant dead code, duplication across 3 layers, and inconsistencies. The codebase evolved from a monolithic AnalyzerWorkflow to a task-based interface (TaskWorkflow), but the old code was never removed.
     6â†’
     7â†’---
     8â†’
     9â†’## 1. Subcommand Review (All 5 are correct and necessary)
    10â†’
    11â†’All subcommands produce correct help output (verified by running `--help`):
    12â†’
    13â†’| Subcommand | Positional | Key Options | Purpose |
    14â†’|---|---|---|---|
    15â†’| `collect` | `metadata` | `--op-filter`, `--type` | Extract test shapes from model metadata |
    16â†’| `augment` | `input_dir` | `--augment-seed` | Generate train/val shapes via log-normal distribution |
    17â†’| `search` | `input_dir` | `--pattern`, `--config`, `--workers` | Generate tile configurations per split |
    18â†’| `compile` | `input_dir` | `--pattern`, `-p/--config`, `--workers`, `--timeout`, `--run-worker`, `--limit` | Generate MLIR + compile to vmfb |
    19â†’| `all` | `metadata` | All of the above combined | Full pipeline: collect->augment->search->compile |
    20â†’
    21â†’Each represents a distinct pipeline stage. `all` is a convenience chaining the other 4. No duplication among subcommands.
    22â†’
    23â†’---
    24â†’
    25â†’## 2. Dead Code & Duplication Found
    26â†’
    27â†’### A. AnalyzerWorkflow is unreachable (DEAD)
    28â†’
    29â†’**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/analyzer.py` (184 lines)
    30â†’
    31â†’Why it's unreachable:
    32â†’- `can_handle()` checks `getattr(args, 'metadata', None) is not None` + no `dataset_dir`
    33â†’- `metadata` is only defined as positional arg on `collect`/`all` subparsers, not the main parser
    34â†’- When `collect`/`all` is used, TaskWorkflow matches first (higher priority in registry)
    35â†’- Without a subcommand, validator Rule 3 rejects ("At least one of: task subcommand or --dataset-dir is required")
    36â†’- Also references `args.gen_fingerprint` and `args.gen_pattern` which don't exist in parser
    37â†’
    38â†’### B. Dead methods in TaskWorkflow (~200 lines)
    39â†’
    40â†’**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/task.py`
    41â†’
    42â†’Active path is `_execute_task()` (line 274) dispatching to `cli/tasks.py`. These are orphaned:
    43â†’
    44â†’| Method | Lines | Replaced By |
    45â†’|---|---|---|
    46â†’| `_check_task_inputs()` | 202-272 | Input checking in task handlers |
    47â†’| `_execute_collect()` | 306-362 | `tasks.run_collect_task()` |
    48â†’| `_execute_augment()` | 364-415 | `tasks.run_augment_task()` |
    49â†’| `_execute_search()` | 417-459 | `tasks.run_search_task()` |
    50â†’| `_execute_compile()` | 461-503 | `tasks.run_compile_task()` |
    51â†’
    52â†’This also makes these imports in task.py dead:
    53â†’- `run_pattern_phase`, `run_augmentation_phase`, `run_search_space_phase_wrapper`, `run_mlir_phase_wrapper`, `run_compile_phase_wrapper`
    54â†’- `filter_stats_by_op`, `filter_stats_by_type`, `save_patterns_json`
    55â†’- `validate_dataset_dir`, `run_pipeline_for_directory`
    56â†’
    57â†’### C. Dead functions in pipeline.py
    58â†’
    59â†’**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/pipeline.py`
    60â†’
    61â†’- `run_splits_pipeline()` (lines 292-332) - defined but never called in production
    62â†’- `_compact_report_json` alias (line 59) - unnecessary backwards-compat alias
    63â†’
    64â†’### D. Dead phase wrappers in phases.py
    65â†’
    66â†’**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/phases.py`
    67â†’
    68â†’Only called by dead AnalyzerWorkflow or dead `_execute_*` methods:
    69â†’
    70â†’| Function | Lines | Status |
    71â†’|---|---|---|
    72â†’| `run_fingerprint_phase()` | 52-162 | DEAD |
    73â†’| `run_pattern_phase()` | 165-241 | DEAD |
    74â†’| `run_search_space_phase_wrapper()` | 244-318 | DEAD |
    75â†’| `run_mlir_phase_wrapper()` | 321-375 | DEAD |
    76â†’| `run_compile_phase_wrapper()` | 378-482 | DEAD |
    77â†’
    78â†’Still alive and needed:
    79â†’- `extract_shapes_from_stats()` (line 485) - used by `run_augment_task()` in tasks.py AND tests
    80â†’- `run_augmentation_phase()` (line 524) - used by 3 integration tests (not production code, but useful)
    81â†’- `PhaseResult` dataclass - used by `run_augmentation_phase()`
    82â†’
    83â†’### E. Unused import in tasks.py
    84â†’
    85â†’**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/tasks.py` line 33
    86â†’
    87â†’`run_augmentation_phase` is imported but never called (the augment handler uses `generate_dataset`/`write_shapes_file` directly).
    88â†’
    89â†’### F. Parser inconsistencies
    90â†’
    91â†’**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/parser.py`
    92â†’
    93â†’1. `search` subparser missing `--max-configs` (hardcoded to 100000 in `run_search_task`)
    94â†’2. `--config` has `-p` short flag on `compile` and `all` but not `search`
    95â†’
    96â†’---
    97â†’
    98â†’## 3. Cleanup Steps
    99â†’
   100â†’### Step 1: Delete AnalyzerWorkflow + its tests
   101â†’
   102â†’**Delete files:**
   103â†’- `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/analyzer.py`
   104â†’- `tests/cli/workflows/test_analyzer_workflow.py`
   105â†’
   106â†’**Modify:**
   107â†’- `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/__init__.py` - remove `AnalyzerWorkflow` import/export
   108â†’- `script/dataset_generator/cli/main.py` - remove from import and `build_workflow_registry()`
   109â†’- `script/dataset_generator/cli/__init__.py` - remove `AnalyzerWorkflow` from imports/`__all__`
   110â†’- `tests/cli/test_main_integration.py` - remove AnalyzerWorkflow from registry tests (lines 57-91)
   111â†’- `tests/cli/workflows/test_task_workflow.py` - remove AnalyzerWorkflow from registry ordering test (line 511-519)
   112â†’
   113â†’### Step 2: Remove dead methods from TaskWorkflow
   114â†’
   115â†’**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/task.py`
   116â†’
   117â†’- Delete methods: `_check_task_inputs`, `_execute_collect`, `_execute_augment`, `_execute_search`, `_execute_compile` (lines 202-503)
   118â†’- Remove dead imports at top (lines 16-34): keep only what `_execute_task`, `__init__`, and `execute` need
   119â†’
   120â†’After cleanup, task.py imports should only be:
   121â†’```python
   122â†’import sys
   123â†’from pathlib import Path
   124â†’from argparse import Namespace
   125â†’from typing import List
   126â†’from ...formatting import print_final_summary
   127â†’```
   128â†’
   129â†’### Step 3: Remove dead phase wrappers from phases.py
   130â†’
   131â†’**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/phases.py`
   132â†’
   133â†’- Delete: `run_fingerprint_phase`, `run_pattern_phase`, `run_search_space_phase_wrapper`, `run_mlir_phase_wrapper`, `run_compile_phase_wrapper` (lines 52-482)
   134â†’- Keep: `PhaseResult`, `extract_shapes_from_stats`, `run_augmentation_phase`
   135â†’- Remove dead imports: `re`, `Counter`, `HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer`, `generate_tile_configs_from_statistics`, `generate_all_mlir_files`, `build_iree_compile_cmd_template`, `compile_all_files`, `generate_pattern_compile_report`, `split_pattern_report`, `print_phase_header`, `print_phase_footer`
   136â†’
   137â†’**Delete most of tests/cli/test_phases.py:**
   138â†’- Keep `TestPhaseResult` (tests PhaseResult dataclass, still alive)
   139â†’- Delete `TestFingerprintPhase`, `TestPatternPhase`, `TestSearchSpacePhase`, `TestMLIRPhase`, `TestCompilePhase`, `TestPhaseOrchestration`
   140â†’
   141â†’### Step 4: Clean up pipeline.py
   142â†’
   143â†’**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/pipeline.py`
   144â†’
   145â†’- Delete `run_splits_pipeline()` (lines 292-332)
   146â†’- Delete `_compact_report_json` alias (line 59)
   147â†’- Keep: `compact_report_json`, `run_search_space_phase`, `run_mlir_phase`, `run_compile_phase`, `run_pipeline_for_directory`, `validate_dataset_dir`, `resolve_pattern_source` (used by live DatasetSplitsWorkflow)
   148â†’
   149â†’**Delete from tests/script/test_augmentor_integration.py:**
   150â†’- Delete class `TestRunSplitsPipeline` (lines 380+) which tests `run_splits_pipeline`
   151â†’
   152â†’### Step 5: Fix unused import in tasks.py
   153â†’
   154â†’**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/tasks.py` line 33
   155â†’- Change `from .phases import extract_shapes_from_stats, run_augmentation_phase` to `from .phases import extract_shapes_from_stats`
   156â†’
   157â†’### Step 6: Fix parser inconsistencies
   158â†’
   159â†’**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/parser.py`
   160â†’
   161â†’- Add `--max-configs` to `search` subparser (via `add_search_options(search_parser)`)
   162â†’- Add `-p` short flag to `search` subparser's `--config`
   163â†’
   164â†’### Step 7: Update cli/__init__.py exports
   165â†’
   166â†’**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/__init__.py`
   167â†’
   168â†’Remove dead re-exports:
   169â†’- `run_fingerprint_phase`, `run_pattern_phase`, `run_search_space_phase_wrapper`, `run_mlir_phase_wrapper`, `run_compile_phase_wrapper`
   170â†’- `AnalyzerWorkflow`
   171â†’- `run_splits_pipeline`
   172â†’
   173â†’---
   174â†’
   175â†’## 4. Summary of all files touched
   176â†’
   177â†’| File | Action |
   178â†’|---|---|
   179â†’| `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/analyzer.py` | **DELETE** |
   180â†’| `tests/cli/workflows/test_analyzer_workflow.py` | **DELETE** |
   181â†’| `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/__init__.py` | Remove AnalyzerWorkflow |
   182â†’| `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/task.py` | Remove 5 dead methods + dead imports (~300 lines) |
   183â†’| `script/dataset_generator/cli/main.py` | Remove AnalyzerWorkflow from registry |
   184â†’| `script/dataset_generator/cli/phases.py` | Remove 5 dead phase wrappers (~430 lines) |
   185â†’| `script/dataset_generator/cli/pipeline.py` | Remove `run_splits_pipeline` + alias (~42 lines) |
   186â†’| `script/dataset_generator/cli/tasks.py` | Remove unused import |
   187â†’| `script/dataset_generator/cli/parser.py` | Add --max-configs to search, add -p to search --config |
   188â†’| `script/dataset_generator/cli/__init__.py` | Remove dead re-exports |
   189â†’| `tests/cli/test_phases.py` | Delete 6 test classes, keep TestPhaseResult |
   190â†’| `tests/cli/test_main_integration.py` | Remove AnalyzerWorkflow from registry tests |
   191â†’| `tests/cli/workflows/test_task_workflow.py` | Remove AnalyzerWorkflow from registry ordering |
   192â†’| `tests/script/test_augmentor_integration.py` | Delete TestRunSplitsPipeline class |
   193â†’
   194â†’**Estimated removal: ~800 lines of dead code + ~200 lines of dead tests**
   195â†’
   196â†’---
   197â†’
   198â†’## 5. Verification
   199â†’
   200â†’```sh
   201â†’# After each step, run tests
   202â†’pytest tests/ -x
   203â†’
   204â†’# Final verification: all subcommands still work
   205â†’python3 script/dataset_generator.py --help

file.content:
# CLI Subcommand Review: Findings & Cleanup Plan

## Context

Reviewed all 5 subcommands (`collect`, `augment`, `search`, `compile`, `all`) plus 2 legacy workflows (`--dataset-dir` DatasetSplitsWorkflow, AnalyzerWorkflow). Found significant dead code, duplication across 3 layers, and inconsistencies. The codebase evolved from a monolithic AnalyzerWorkflow to a task-based interface (TaskWorkflow), but the old code was never removed.

---

## 1. Subcommand Review (All 5 are correct and necessary)

All subcommands produce correct help output (verified by running `--help`):

| Subcommand | Positional | Key Options | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `collect` | `metadata` | `--op-filter`, `--type` | Extract test shapes from model metadata |
| `augment` | `input_dir` | `--augment-seed` | Generate train/val shapes via log-normal distribution |
| `search` | `input_dir` | `--pattern`, `--config`, `--workers` | Generate tile configurations per split |
| `compile` | `input_dir` | `--pattern`, `-p/--config`, `--workers`, `--timeout`, `--run-worker`, `--limit` | Generate MLIR + compile to vmfb |
| `all` | `metadata` | All of the above combined | Full pipeline: collect->augment->search->compile |

Each represents a distinct pipeline stage. `all` is a convenience chaining the other 4. No duplication among subcommands.

---

## 2. Dead Code & Duplication Found

### A. AnalyzerWorkflow is unreachable (DEAD)

**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/analyzer.py` (184 lines)

Why it's unreachable:
- `can_handle()` checks `getattr(args, 'metadata', None) is not None` + no `dataset_dir`
- `metadata` is only defined as positional arg on `collect`/`all` subparsers, not the main parser
- When `collect`/`all` is used, TaskWorkflow matches first (higher priority in registry)
- Without a subcommand, validator Rule 3 rejects ("At least one of: task subcommand or --dataset-dir is required")
- Also references `args.gen_fingerprint` and `args.gen_pattern` which don't exist in parser

### B. Dead methods in TaskWorkflow (~200 lines)

**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/task.py`

Active path is `_execute_task()` (line 274) dispatching to `cli/tasks.py`. These are orphaned:

| Method | Lines | Replaced By |
|---|---|---|
| `_check_task_inputs()` | 202-272 | Input checking in task handlers |
| `_execute_collect()` | 306-362 | `tasks.run_collect_task()` |
| `_execute_augment()` | 364-415 | `tasks.run_augment_task()` |
| `_execute_search()` | 417-459 | `tasks.run_search_task()` |
| `_execute_compile()` | 461-503 | `tasks.run_compile_task()` |

This also makes these imports in task.py dead:
- `run_pattern_phase`, `run_augmentation_phase`, `run_search_space_phase_wrapper`, `run_mlir_phase_wrapper`, `run_compile_phase_wrapper`
- `filter_stats_by_op`, `filter_stats_by_type`, `save_patterns_json`
- `validate_dataset_dir`, `run_pipeline_for_directory`

### C. Dead functions in pipeline.py

**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/pipeline.py`

- `run_splits_pipeline()` (lines 292-332) - defined but never called in production
- `_compact_report_json` alias (line 59) - unnecessary backwards-compat alias

### D. Dead phase wrappers in phases.py

**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/phases.py`

Only called by dead AnalyzerWorkflow or dead `_execute_*` methods:

| Function | Lines | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `run_fingerprint_phase()` | 52-162 | DEAD |
| `run_pattern_phase()` | 165-241 | DEAD |
| `run_search_space_phase_wrapper()` | 244-318 | DEAD |
| `run_mlir_phase_wrapper()` | 321-375 | DEAD |
| `run_compile_phase_wrapper()` | 378-482 | DEAD |

Still alive and needed:
- `extract_shapes_from_stats()` (line 485) - used by `run_augment_task()` in tasks.py AND tests
- `run_augmentation_phase()` (line 524) - used by 3 integration tests (not production code, but useful)
- `PhaseResult` dataclass - used by `run_augmentation_phase()`

### E. Unused import in tasks.py

**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/tasks.py` line 33

`run_augmentation_phase` is imported but never called (the augment handler uses `generate_dataset`/`write_shapes_file` directly).

### F. Parser inconsistencies

**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/parser.py`

1. `search` subparser missing `--max-configs` (hardcoded to 100000 in `run_search_task`)
2. `--config` has `-p` short flag on `compile` and `all` but not `search`

---

## 3. Cleanup Steps

### Step 1: Delete AnalyzerWorkflow + its tests

**Delete files:**
- `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/analyzer.py`
- `tests/cli/workflows/test_analyzer_workflow.py`

**Modify:**
- `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/__init__.py` - remove `AnalyzerWorkflow` import/export
- `script/dataset_generator/cli/main.py` - remove from import and `build_workflow_registry()`
- `script/dataset_generator/cli/__init__.py` - remove `AnalyzerWorkflow` from imports/`__all__`
- `tests/cli/test_main_integration.py` - remove AnalyzerWorkflow from registry tests (lines 57-91)
- `tests/cli/workflows/test_task_workflow.py` - remove AnalyzerWorkflow from registry ordering test (line 511-519)

### Step 2: Remove dead methods from TaskWorkflow

**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/task.py`

- Delete methods: `_check_task_inputs`, `_execute_collect`, `_execute_augment`, `_execute_search`, `_execute_compile` (lines 202-503)
- Remove dead imports at top (lines 16-34): keep only what `_execute_task`, `__init__`, and `execute` need

After cleanup, task.py imports should only be:
```python
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from argparse import Namespace
from typing import List
from ...formatting import print_final_summary
```

### Step 3: Remove dead phase wrappers from phases.py

**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/phases.py`

- Delete: `run_fingerprint_phase`, `run_pattern_phase`, `run_search_space_phase_wrapper`, `run_mlir_phase_wrapper`, `run_compile_phase_wrapper` (lines 52-482)
- Keep: `PhaseResult`, `extract_shapes_from_stats`, `run_augmentation_phase`
- Remove dead imports: `re`, `Counter`, `HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer`, `generate_tile_configs_from_statistics`, `generate_all_mlir_files`, `build_iree_compile_cmd_template`, `compile_all_files`, `generate_pattern_compile_report`, `split_pattern_report`, `print_phase_header`, `print_phase_footer`

**Delete most of tests/cli/test_phases.py:**
- Keep `TestPhaseResult` (tests PhaseResult dataclass, still alive)
- Delete `TestFingerprintPhase`, `TestPatternPhase`, `TestSearchSpacePhase`, `TestMLIRPhase`, `TestCompilePhase`, `TestPhaseOrchestration`

### Step 4: Clean up pipeline.py

**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/pipeline.py`

- Delete `run_splits_pipeline()` (lines 292-332)
- Delete `_compact_report_json` alias (line 59)
- Keep: `compact_report_json`, `run_search_space_phase`, `run_mlir_phase`, `run_compile_phase`, `run_pipeline_for_directory`, `validate_dataset_dir`, `resolve_pattern_source` (used by live DatasetSplitsWorkflow)

**Delete from tests/script/test_augmentor_integration.py:**
- Delete class `TestRunSplitsPipeline` (lines 380+) which tests `run_splits_pipeline`

### Step 5: Fix unused import in tasks.py

**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/tasks.py` line 33
- Change `from .phases import extract_shapes_from_stats, run_augmentation_phase` to `from .phases import extract_shapes_from_stats`

### Step 6: Fix parser inconsistencies

**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/parser.py`

- Add `--max-configs` to `search` subparser (via `add_search_options(search_parser)`)
- Add `-p` short flag to `search` subparser's `--config`

### Step 7: Update cli/__init__.py exports

**File:** `script/dataset_generator/cli/__init__.py`

Remove dead re-exports:
- `run_fingerprint_phase`, `run_pattern_phase`, `run_search_space_phase_wrapper`, `run_mlir_phase_wrapper`, `run_compile_phase_wrapper`
- `AnalyzerWorkflow`
- `run_splits_pipeline`

---

## 4. Summary of all files touched

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/analyzer.py` | **DELETE** |
| `tests/cli/workflows/test_analyzer_workflow.py` | **DELETE** |
| `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/__init__.py` | Remove AnalyzerWorkflow |
| `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/task.py` | Remove 5 dead methods + dead imports (~300 lines) |
| `script/dataset_generator/cli/main.py` | Remove AnalyzerWorkflow from registry |
| `script/dataset_generator/cli/phases.py` | Remove 5 dead phase wrappers (~430 lines) |
| `script/dataset_generator/cli/pipeline.py` | Remove `run_splits_pipeline` + alias (~42 lines) |
| `script/dataset_generator/cli/tasks.py` | Remove unused import |
| `script/dataset_generator/cli/parser.py` | Add --max-configs to search, add -p to search --config |
| `script/dataset_generator/cli/__init__.py` | Remove dead re-exports |
| `tests/cli/test_phases.py` | Delete 6 test classes, keep TestPhaseResult |
| `tests/cli/test_main_integration.py` | Remove AnalyzerWorkflow from registry tests |
| `tests/cli/workflows/test_task_workflow.py` | Remove AnalyzerWorkflow from registry ordering |
| `tests/script/test_augmentor_integration.py` | Delete TestRunSplitsPipeline class |

**Estimated removal: ~800 lines of dead code + ~200 lines of dead tests**

---

## 5. Verification

```sh
# After each step, run tests
pytest tests/ -x

# Final verification: all subcommands still work
python3 script/dataset_generator.py --help
python3 script/dataset_generator.py collect --help
python3 script/dataset_generator.py augment --help
python3 script/dataset_generator.py search --help   # should now show --max-configs
python3 script/dataset_generator.py compile --help
python3 script/dataset_generator.py all --help

# Verify --dataset-dir legacy mode still works
python3 script/dataset_generator.py --dataset-dir /nonexistent --op-filter matmul --gen-search-space 2>&1 | head -5
# Should show error about directory not found (not argument error)
```

message.content:
Your task is to create a detailed summary of the conversation so far, paying close attention to the user's explicit requests and your previous actions.
This summary should be thorough in capturing technical details, code patterns, and architectural decisions that would be essential for continuing development work without losing context.

Before providing your final summary, wrap your analysis in <analysis> tags to organize your thoughts and ensure you've covered all necessary points. In your analysis process:

1. Chronologically analyze each message and section of the conversation. For each section thoroughly identify:
   - The user's explicit requests and intents
   - Your approach to addressing the user's requests
   - Key decisions, technical concepts and code patterns
   - Specific details like:
     - file names
     - full code snippets
     - function signatures
     - file edits
  - Errors that you ran into and how you fixed them
  - Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
2. Double-check for technical accuracy and completeness, addressing each required element thoroughly.

Your summary should include the following sections:

1. Primary Request and Intent: Capture all of the user's explicit requests and intents in detail
2. Key Technical Concepts: List all important technical concepts, technologies, and frameworks discussed.
3. Files and Code Sections: Enumerate specific files and code sections examined, modified, or created. Pay special attention to the most recent messages and include full code snippets where applicable and include a summary of why this file read or edit is important.
4. Errors and fixes: List all errors that you ran into, and how you fixed them. Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
5. Problem Solving: Document problems solved and any ongoing troubleshooting efforts.
6. All user messages: List ALL user messages that are not tool results. These are critical for understanding the users' feedback and changing intent.
6. Pending Tasks: Outline any pending tasks that you have explicitly been asked to work on.
7. Current Work: Describe in detail precisely what was being worked on immediately before this summary request, paying special attention to the most recent messages from both user and assistant. Include file names and code snippets where applicable.

<analysis>
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. The user provided a detailed plan for CLI cleanup of an IREE MLIR dataset generator project. The plan identified dead code, duplication, and inconsistencies across the codebase.

2. I executed Steps 1-7 of the plan:
   - Step 1: Deleted AnalyzerWorkflow + tests, updated references
   - Step 2: Removed dead methods from TaskWorkflow (task.py)
   - Step 3: Removed dead phase wrappers from phases.py
   - Step 4: Cleaned up pipeline.py (removed run_splits_pipeline + alias)
   - Step 5: Fixed unused import in tasks.py
   - Step 6: Fixed parser inconsistencies (added --max-configs and -p to search subparser)
   - Step 7: Updated cli/__init__.py exports

3. After completing all 7 steps, tests went from 798 â†’ 780 (18 dead tests removed).

4. User asked two questions:
   - "what is --max-configs? for total configs? or for single pattern?" - I answered: per pattern
   - "I told you to remove legacy code like --dataset-dir is redundant." - User was frustrated I didn't remove --dataset-dir/DatasetSplitsWorkflow

5. I proposed removing --dataset-dir and asked if user wanted to proceed.

6. User said: "go through all tasks and subcommands. make sure no legacy cmd/features have the same functionality."

7. I launched 3 explore agents to audit all legacy code paths, test dependencies, and utility scripts.

8. User sent additional messages while agents were running:
   - "clean up test cases (remove) if necessary"
   - "make a thorough plan ! Think harder"

9. I entered plan mode and began thorough exploration:
   - Read validators.py, main.py, pipeline.py, base.py, parser.py, test_validators.py, test_pattern_id_filter.py
   - Identified complete redundancy map between --dataset-dir/DatasetSplitsWorkflow and subcommand interface

10. I was in the process of writing the plan file when the summary was requested. The old plan file still contains the PREVIOUS plan (Steps 1-7 already executed).

Key findings from the audit:
- DatasetSplitsWorkflow + --dataset-dir + phase flags (--gen-search-space, --gen-mlir, --compile-mlir) are ALL redundant with subcommand interface
- run_pipeline_for_directory() and its helper phase functions (run_search_space_phase, run_mlir_phase, run_compile_phase) are only used by the legacy path
- validate_dataset_dir() and resolve_pattern_source() are legacy-only
- Top-level parser options (--max-configs, --workers, --timeout, --run-worker, -p/--config, -o, --op-filter, --type) duplicate what subparsers define
- Multiple test files have legacy-only test classes that should be removed
- The `all` subparser needs add_base_options() added since it currently relies on the main parser's --op-filter
- compare_l2_configs.py and sweep_l2_targets.py (untracked scripts) use legacy flags

Current state after Steps 1-7:
- AnalyzerWorkflow fully removed
- Dead methods in TaskWorkflow removed
- Dead phase wrappers in phases.py removed
- run_splits_pipeline removed from pipeline.py
- Unused import fixed in tasks.py
- Parser inconsistencies fixed
- cli/__init__.py exports cleaned up
- 780 tests passing

What remains to be done (the NEW plan not yet written):
- Delete DatasetSplitsWorkflow + its test file
- Remove --dataset-dir, --gen-search-space, --gen-mlir, --compile-mlir from parser
- Remove top-level duplicate options from parser
- Add add_base_options to all_parser
- Simplify main.py (remove DatasetSplitsWorkflow references, simplify output defaulting)
- Remove legacy validator rules
- Remove run_pipeline_for_directory, run_search_space_phase, run_mlir_phase, run_compile_phase, validate_dataset_dir, resolve_pattern_source from pipeline.py
- Remove/update legacy tests across multiple test files
- Update cli/__init__.py exports
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user provided a detailed cleanup plan for an IREE MLIR dataset generator CLI codebase. The initial plan had 7 steps to remove dead code from AnalyzerWorkflow, TaskWorkflow dead methods, phase wrappers, pipeline functions, and fix parser inconsistencies. After I executed all 7 steps, the user pointed out I should ALSO have identified and removed the `--dataset-dir` / `DatasetSplitsWorkflow` legacy path as redundant with the modern subcommand interface (`collect`, `augment`, `search`, `compile`, `all`). The user explicitly said: "I told you to remove legacy code like --dataset-dir is redundant" and then "go through all tasks and subcommands. make sure no legacy cmd/features have the same functionality." and "clean up test cases (remove) if necessary" and "make a thorough plan ! Think harder". The user wants a thorough plan to remove ALL remaining legacy/redundant code, including the `--dataset-dir` workflow and all associated dead code paths.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - IREE MLIR dataset generator for RISC-V FPGA benchmarking
   - CLI with subcommand-based interface: `collect`, `augment`, `search`, `compile`, `all`
   - Legacy `--dataset-dir` mode with phase flags (`--gen-search-space`, `--gen-mlir`, `--compile-mlir`)
   - WorkflowRegistry pattern dispatching to DatasetSplitsWorkflow or TaskWorkflow
   - argparse subparsers with shared namespace between main parser and subparsers
   - TDD development practices (project uses pytest, Redâ†’Greenâ†’Refactor)
   - Pipeline stages: fingerprint â†’ pattern â†’ search space â†’ MLIR gen â†’ compile â†’ benchmark
   - No external Python dependencies (stdlib only, TOML via tomllib)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   **ALREADY COMPLETED (Steps 1-7):**

   - `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/analyzer.py` â€” **DELETED** (184 lines, unreachable AnalyzerWorkflow)
   - `tests/cli/workflows/test_analyzer_workflow.py` â€” **DELETED** (156 lines)
   
   - `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/__init__.py` â€” Removed AnalyzerWorkflow import/export:
     ```python
     # Now contains:
     from .base import Workflow, WorkflowRegistry
     from .dataset_splits import DatasetSplitsWorkflow
     from .task import TaskWorkflow
     __all__ = ['Workflow', 'WorkflowRegistry', 'DatasetSplitsWorkflow', 'TaskWorkflow']
     ```

   - `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/task.py` â€” Removed 5 dead methods (~300 lines): `_check_task_inputs`, `_execute_collect`, `_execute_augment`, `_execute_search`, `_execute_compile` and dead imports. Now only imports:
     ```python
     import sys
     from pathlib import Path
     from argparse import Namespace, ArgumentParser
     from typing import List
     ```

   - `script/dataset_generator/cli/phases.py` â€” Rewrote to keep only `PhaseResult`, `extract_shapes_from_stats`, `run_augmentation_phase` (~120 lines, down from ~573). Removed 5 dead phase wrappers and heavy imports.

   - `script/dataset_generator/cli/pipeline.py` â€” Removed `run_splits_pipeline()` and `_compact_report_json` alias. Still contains: `compact_report_json`, `run_search_space_phase`, `run_mlir_phase`, `run_compile_phase`, `run_pipeline_for_directory`, `validate_dataset_dir`, `resolve_pattern_source`.

   - `script/dataset_generator/cli/tasks.py` â€” Changed import from `from .phases import extract_shapes_from_stats, run_augmentation_phase` to `from .phases import extract_shapes_from_stats`

   - `script/dataset_generator/cli/parser.py` â€” Added `add_search_options(search_parser)` and changed search's `--config` to have `-p` short flag:
     ```python
     search_parser.add_argument(
         '-p', '--config',
         default='config/AX45_1024.toml',
         help='IREE compiler config for spill probing (default: config/AX45_1024.toml)'
     )
     ```

   - `script/dataset_generator/cli/__init__.py` â€” Removed dead re-exports (AnalyzerWorkflow, 5 phase wrappers, run_splits_pipeline). Current state still exports: `DatasetSplitsWorkflow`, `run_search_space_phase`, `run_mlir_phase`, `run_compile_phase`, `run_pipeline_for_directory`, `validate_dataset_dir`, `resolve_pattern_source`.

   - `script/dataset_generator/cli/main.py` â€” Removed AnalyzerWorkflow from import and registry. Current state:
     ```python
     from .workflows import WorkflowRegistry, DatasetSplitsWorkflow, TaskWorkflow
     def build_workflow_registry():
         registry = WorkflowRegistry()
         registry.register(DatasetSplitsWorkflow)  # Priority 1: --dataset-dir
         registry.register(TaskWorkflow)           # Priority 2: --task
         return registry
     ```

   - `tests/cli/test_phases.py` â€” Rewrote to keep only `TestPhaseResult` class (3 tests)
   - `tests/cli/test_main_integration.py` â€” Rewrote registry tests to use TaskWorkflow instead of AnalyzerWorkflow
   - `tests/cli/workflows/test_task_workflow.py` â€” Updated priority test to only check DatasetSplitsWorkflow < TaskWorkflow
   - `tests/script/test_augmentor_integration.py` â€” Removed `TestRunSplitsPipeline` class

   **FILES READ BUT NOT YET MODIFIED (for the new plan):**

   - `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/dataset_splits.py` (131 lines) â€” The legacy DatasetSplitsWorkflow. Calls `validate_dataset_dir()`, `resolve_pattern_source()`, `build_stats_from_shape_source()`, `run_pipeline_for_directory()` for each split. THIS IS THE MAIN TARGET FOR REMOVAL.

   - `script/dataset_generator/cli/validators.py` â€” Contains 5 validation rules. Rules 1-2 are legacy-only (dataset_dir requires op_filter, dataset_dir requires phase flag). Rule 3 checks for subcommand OR dataset_dir. Rules 4-5 are modern (--pattern validation).

   - `script/dataset_generator/cli/pipeline.py` â€” Current state has: `compact_report_json` (KEEP - used by tasks.py), `run_search_space_phase` (LEGACY), `run_mlir_phase` (LEGACY), `run_compile_phase` (LEGACY), `run_pipeline_for_directory` (LEGACY), `validate_dataset_dir` (LEGACY), `resolve_pattern_source` (LEGACY).

   - `script/dataset_generator/cli/parser.py` â€” Still has legacy args at top level: `--dataset-dir` (lines 135-142), phase flags (lines 144-162), top-level duplicates of --max-configs/--workers/--timeout/--run-worker/-p/--config (lines 164-171). The `all` subparser does NOT have `add_base_options()` â€” it relies on the main parser's `--op-filter`.

   - `script/dataset_generator/cli/workflows/base.py` â€” Workflow Protocol and WorkflowRegistry classes (126 lines). Registry is over-engineered for one workflow but not strictly "legacy."

   - `tests/cli/workflows/test_dataset_splits_workflow.py` â€” Entire file is legacy-only (5 tests)
   - `tests/cli/test_validators.py` â€” `TestDatasetDirValidation` (3 tests) is legacy-only. `TestActionRequirement` and `TestValidationResult` are shared/modern.
   - `tests/cli/test_main_integration.py` â€” Multiple tests mock DatasetSplitsWorkflow (legacy)
   - `tests/script/test_augmentor_integration.py` â€” Has legacy-only classes: `TestRunPipelineForDirectory`, `TestValidateDatasetDir`, `TestResolvePatternSource`, `TestDatasetDirCLIParsing`, `TestDatasetDirCLIValidation`
   - `tests/script/test_pattern_id_filter.py` â€” `TestPatternsJsonWithPatternId` (2 tests) uses `run_pipeline_for_directory` with phase flags

   - `script/compare_l2_configs.py` (untracked) â€” Uses legacy `--gen-search-space` flag via subprocess
   - `script/sweep_l2_targets.py` (untracked) â€” Uses legacy phase flags via subprocess
   - `script/analyze_spills.py` (untracked) â€” No legacy CLI dependencies

4. Errors and fixes:
   - After Step 1 (deleting AnalyzerWorkflow), tests failed with `ImportError: cannot import name 'AnalyzerWorkflow' from 'dataset_generator.cli.workflows'` because `cli/__init__.py` still imported it. Fixed by also updating `cli/__init__.py` (which was planned for Step 7 but needed immediately).
   - User feedback: "what is --max-configs? for total configs? or for single pattern?" â€” It's per pattern (passed to `generate_tile_configs_from_statistics` as `max_configs`).
   - User feedback: "I told you to remove Legacy code like --dataset-dir is redundant." â€” I should have identified that `--dataset-dir`/DatasetSplitsWorkflow duplicates the subcommand interface and included it in the cleanup plan.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Successfully removed ~1000 lines of dead production code and ~200 lines of dead tests in Steps 1-7
   - Tests went from 798 â†’ 780 (all passing)
   - Identified that `--dataset-dir` + `DatasetSplitsWorkflow` is fully replaceable by `search` + `compile` subcommands
   - Discovered the `all` subparser doesn't define `--op-filter` â€” it relies on the main parser's definition, so removing top-level `add_base_options(parser)` requires adding `add_base_options(all_parser)`
   - Identified that `run_pipeline_for_directory()` and its helper phase functions in pipeline.py are only reachable from the legacy DatasetSplitsWorkflow path

6. All user messages:
   - "Implement the following plan: [detailed 7-step cleanup plan for AnalyzerWorkflow, dead methods, phase wrappers, pipeline cleanup, unused imports, parser fixes, exports]"
   - "what is --max-configs? for total configs? or for single pattern? I told you to remove Legacycode like --dataset-dir is redundant."
   - "go through all tasks and subcommands. make sure no legacy cmd/fetures have the same functionality."
   - "clean up test cases (remove) if necessary"
   - "make a thorough plan ! Think harder"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Write and execute a new plan to remove ALL remaining legacy/redundant code:
     - Delete `DatasetSplitsWorkflow` + `test_dataset_splits_workflow.py`
     - Remove `--dataset-dir`, `--gen-search-space`, `--gen-mlir`, `--compile-mlir` from parser
     - Remove top-level duplicate options from parser (--max-configs, --workers, --timeout, --run-worker, -p/--config, -o, --op-filter, --type)
     - Add `add_base_options(all_parser)` so `all` subcommand has `--op-filter` and `--type`
     - Simplify `main.py` (remove DatasetSplitsWorkflow references, simplify output defaulting)
     - Remove legacy validator rules from `validators.py`
     - Remove `run_pipeline_for_directory`, `run_search_space_phase`, `run_mlir_phase`, `run_compile_phase`, `validate_dataset_dir`, `resolve_pattern_source` from `pipeline.py`
     - Update `cli/__init__.py` exports
     - Remove/update legacy test classes across test files
     - Make subcommand required (error early if no subcommand given)

8. Current Work:
   I was in **plan mode**, having completed Phase 1 (exploration) and Phase 2 (design via explore agents). I had read all the critical files and gathered a complete redundancy map. I was about to write the new plan to the plan file at `/home/nick/.claude/plans/mutable-bouncing-book.md` (which still contains the OLD plan from Steps 1-7 that were already executed). The user's most recent explicit requests were: "go through all tasks and subcommands. make sure no legacy cmd/features have the same functionality." and "clean up test cases (remove) if necessary" and "make a thorough plan ! Think harder".

9. Optional Next Step:
   Write the new plan to the plan file, covering all the items in Pending Tasks above, then call `ExitPlanMode` for user approval. The plan should detail every file to delete, modify, and every test to remove or rewrite. After approval, execute the plan step by step, running `pytest tests/ -x` after each step. This directly addresses the user's request to "make a thorough plan" to ensure "no legacy cmd/features have the same functionality."
</summary>
